methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

data.narrative:
Watershed is a Series G climate and energy company founded by Taylor Francis, currently carrying a post-money valuation of $1.8B after raising $200M+. The valuation exercise references a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—TAE Technologies ($6.0B Series G), Helion ($5.4B Series F), Form Energy ($3.5B Series G), and Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B late stage)—supplemented by a broader universe of eight private climate-tech companies and one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods are applicable here. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.25x Series G step-up multiplier to the $1.8B base, implies $2.2B. Peer-median methodology across the four-company comp set yields $4.5B, with a range spanning $3.3B to $6.0B. The wide dispersion reflects stage and business-model variation within the climate tech sector at late-stage rounds.

Synthesizing both methods produces a valuation range of $1.9B (low) to $5.1B (high), with a median estimate of $3.4B. The current $1.8B valuation sits materially below peer-median levels, suggesting either a more conservative capital structure, higher execution risk relative to comps, or earlier revenue/commercialization traction. Primary uncertainties include the timing and structure of the next funding event and the sustainability of comparable valuations in a volatile cleantech sector.
